title: pyexpat/_elementtree: a shared parser's reparse-deferral flag races between SetReparseDeferralEnabled and callProcessor (Parse)
description: The bundled libexpat parser caches reparse-deferral in the plain 1-byte XML_Bool field m_reparseDeferralEnabled. XML_SetReparseDeferralEnabled writes it (xmlparse.c:3035) while callProcessor, reached on every Parse(), reads it (xmlparse.c:1136); neither the pyexpat nor _elementtree wrapper locks the parser, so a parser object shared across threads that mixes SetReparseDeferralEnabled()/flush() with Parse()/close() is a data race on that byte. Value-benign; expat parsers are single-threaded by design.
sites: Modules/pyexpat.c pyexpat_xmlparser_Parse_impl:919 (Python Parse() -> XML_Parse -> callProcessor, no critical section)
race_kind: unsynchronized shared-object field (plain read/write of a bundled-libexpat parser flag; no lock/atomic in the CPython wrapper)
shared_state: XML_ParserStruct.m_reparseDeferralEnabled (per-parser 1-byte XML_Bool reparse-deferral flag), shared when one pyexpat/_elementtree parser object is used by multiple threads
severity: low (value-benign: lone aligned bool byte, both values valid; only perturbs the reparse-deferral heuristic; no crash/UAF from this field). Requires sharing a single parser across threads, which is outside expat's single-threaded contract.
repro_reliability: deterministic (exit 66, <1s; halt_on_error=1). One Parse-driver thread + several SetReparseDeferralEnabled writer threads on a fresh shared pyexpat parser per round.
suggested_fix: No honest single-atomic fix (it would mask only this byte). Either (1) document that pyexpat/_elementtree parser objects are not thread-shareable (status quo, matches libexpat's single-threaded contract), or (2) if pyexpat is to be crash/UB-safe under accidental sharing, wrap the mutating parser methods (Parse/ParseFile/SetReparseDeferralEnabled/flush/feed/close) in a per-parser Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION / PyMutex to serialize them.
notes: Modules/expat/xmlparse.c is third-party bundled libexpat; the PyExpat_ prefix is CPython's expat_config.h symbol renaming. libexpat never synchronizes parser fields because an XML_Parser is single-threaded by design. Unlike TSAN-0005 (dec_hash on an immutable Decimal, where hash() genuinely looks read-only), a pyexpat parser is an explicitly stateful, single-threaded object, so this is closer to expected 'don't share the parser across threads' behavior than an interpreter bug -- likely not worth an individual CPython filing; carried as a low-severity data point for a possible pyexpat per-parser-critical-section decision. This one byte is only the first-touched field; concurrent Parse() races the whole parser struct.
assessment: expected/low-value — bundled single-threaded libexpat; a parser is not thread-shareable by design. Catalog data point, not for individual filing.
fleet02_notes: fleet-02: additional signature face(s) folded in: Modules/expat/xmlparse.c:PyExpat_XML_Parse | Modules/expat/xmlparse.c:PyExpat_XML_Parse
fleet-02: pyexpat self-races (SetReparseDeferralEnabled|self, poolGrow|setContext) folded in -- same bundled-libexpat parser-state class
fleet-02: concurrent XMLParser._setevents on ONE shared parser races Py_XSETREF(events_append)/Py_CLEAR -- same don't-share-the-parser class (TSAN-0022 self-face folded)
